title: Checksum computation and checksums.txt parsing (pure, no network)
action: Add `sha2` as a direct Cargo.toml dependency (already present as a transitive dep at 0.11.0 per Cargo.lock -- adds no new crate to the build, just promotes it; use version "0.11"). Create src/update/checksum.rs (declared via `mod checksum;` in src/update/mod.rs, pub) with three pure functions, no network: (1) `sha256_hex(bytes: &[u8]) -> String` computing the lowercase-hex SHA-256 digest of a byte slice via the sha2 crate; (2) `parse_checksums(body: &str) -> std::collections::HashMap<String, String>` parsing sha256sum-format text (one `<hex-hash>  <filename>` line per entry, exactly the format scripts/generate-checksums.sh produces via `printf '%s  %s\n' "$hash" "$(basename "$f")"` -- two spaces, text mode, no leading `*`, per D8/D10) into a filename->hash map, skipping any line that doesn't split into at least two whitespace-separated tokens rather than erroring (defensive, not strict); (3) `checksum_matches(bytes: &[u8], expected_hex: &str) -> bool` computing sha256_hex(bytes) and comparing case-insensitively against expected_hex. This cell does not download anything or touch the network -- that composition is a later cell. Name the three required unit tests exactly: `sha256_hex_matches_known_vector`, `parse_checksums_extracts_two_entries`, `checksum_matches_is_case_insensitive_and_rejects_wrong_hash` (the verify command checks for these exact names to ensure the fail-closed-relevant cases aren't skipped).

truths: sha256_hex of a known byte sequence matches the well-known SHA-256 test vector for that sequence
parse_checksums on a two-line sha256sum-format body returns both filename->hash entries correctly
checksum_matches returns true for the correct hash and false for a wrong hash, case-insensitively

artifacts.substantive: exports sha256_hex, parse_checksums, checksum_matches with unit tests covering the three truths above, no TODO stubs

prohibitions: No network calls in this cell

verify: test -f src/update/checksum.rs && grep -q '#\[test\]' src/update/checksum.rs && grep -q 'mod checksum' src/update/mod.rs && grep -q 'fn sha256_hex_matches_known_vector' src/update/checksum.rs && grep -q 'fn parse_checksums_extracts_two_entries' src/update/checksum.rs && grep -q 'fn checksum_matches_is_case_insensitive_and_rejects_wrong_hash' src/update/checksum.rs && cargo test --quiet update

trace.outcome: Added sha2 as a direct dependency and created src/update/checksum.rs with sha256_hex, parse_checksums, checksum_matches plus the three named unit tests; declared mod checksum in src/update/mod.rs. No network calls.
